greeting: Morning, Haniel. Calendar's clear — the whole day is yours.
hype: GS financial data gets locked down, the CT Pros QB wizard ships, and the quality-gate engine gets torn apart. Full runway, no excuses. Let's go.

schedule.title: Labor rates + overhead costs (Rony, Giovanne, all roles)
Initial mapping wizard: expense categories → QB accounts
Sandbox-validate all 6 entity QB mappings + upsert/LinkedTxn shapes
Design + installation time benchmarks per project type
S5 · Quality gates — CK1–CK4 inspection engine bug hunt + E2E

tasks.title: Labor rates + overhead costs (Rony, Giovanne, all roles)
tasks.notes: KB 6.4 + 6.5 placeholders. Rony's GSAFI LTDA rate, Giovanne's role + rate, monthly overhead. Required for financial dashboard (t51).

prep.what: Sit with Rony and Giovanne to lock in real labor rates and monthly overhead — live placeholders in KB §6.4–6.5 that every financial calculation reads.
prep.understand: The cost-plus pricing engine, margin rollups, and bonus system all pull from these KB fields. Right now they're zeroes — every financial output in GS-ERP is showing fictional profit.
approach: Open KB §6.4 and §6.5 — list every blank field before you call anyone.
15-min call with Rony: confirm GSAFI LTDA contract amount and Giovanne's role + pay.
Pull monthly overhead line items: rent, utilities, shop insurance, software subscriptions.
Update KB placeholders with the real numbers.
Flag t51 (financial dashboard) that its data dependency is now cleared.

tasks.title: Bonus formula definition (production + paint shop)
tasks.notes: KB 9.1 + 12.13. Private session with Guilherme/Mayara. Production: target days + quality gate. Paint: team-based + labor split rule (equal vs weighted).

prep.what: Get the exact bonus formula from Guilherme and Mayara: the pool/percentage, equal-vs-weighted split for 12 carpenters, and the paint-shop team variant.
prep.understand: `BonusEligibilityCalculator` gates on `actual_days ≤ target_days AND ck2_rework_count==0` but payout amounts are blank — KB §9.1 and §12.13 are both placeholders. Two separate formulas needed: production per-marceneiro pool + split, and the paint-shop variant.
approach: Pull KB §9.1 and §12.13 — list every open question before the session.
Prep a 1-pager: bonus pool (% of project profit?), how is the 12-marceneiro split calculated, what's the paint-shop quality gate threshold.
Run the private session with Guilherme + Mayara — commit concrete numbers to KB.
Note what `BonusEligibilityCalculator` already handles vs what new code (t38) still needs.

tasks.title: Design + installation time benchmarks per project type
tasks.notes: KB 9.1: Track next 10 projects for design time. Interview install crew leads for installation times. Required for capacity planning + accurate scheduling.

prep.what: Build a systematic capture of real design hours and installation hours per project type — the calibration data KB §9.1 needs before capacity planning is meaningful.
prep.understand: `TimeStandard` values are seeded estimates, not measured against GS's actual shop. Design hours vary significantly by type (kitchen >> closet). Without real benchmarks, `ProductionPriorityService` scores on fiction.
approach: Read KB §9.1 — understand the exact format benchmarks need to be in.
Query completed projects for actual design durations (timestamp diffs grouped by `product_type`).
Interview the 3 install crew leads (GKM, IR Woodworking, Caio) for typical hours per type.
Compare actuals vs current `TimeStandard` seeds — note the gaps.
Update KB §9.1 with benchmark data and flag recommended seed adjustments.
resources: Query for project design timestamps grouped by product_type
Install crew lead contacts (GKM, IR Woodworking, Caio Martins)

tasks.title: S5 · Quality gates — CK1–CK4 inspection engine bug hunt + E2E
tasks.notes: find → adversarial-verify (cite Master_flow §) → gated fix → edge-case E2E.

prep.what: Run V1 Polishing Crusade Sector 5 — adversarially hunt CK1–CK4 inspection bugs, verify each against `Master_flow.md`, fix confirmed HIGH/CRITICAL with PHPUnit coverage, write E2E specs for all guard paths.
prep.understand: S1–S4 are done (workflow, estimates, billing, QB). S5 covers `Ck1InspectionService`, `Ck2InspectionService`, fail/pass cascades, rework-round tracking, photo-upload gates, CK4 signature capture, punch list lifecycle. Done when a full round finds zero new HIGH/CRITICAL confirmed bugs.
approach: Read `Master_flow.md` §§ on CK1–CK4 (design verification → fabrication QC → cosmetic → install sign-off).
Walk every guard path: CK1→`clientApproveDrawing`, CK2 incomplete guard, CK3→`awaiting_ck3`, CK4→`signOffCk4`→punch list.
Hunt for: guard bypasses, missing substep transitions, rework count not incrementing, photo not required when KB says it is, wrong permission gates.
Adversarially verify each finding — kill it if you can't cite a `Master_flow.md` section.
For HIGHs: write failing PHPUnit test first, then fix. For E2E: `.local.spec.ts` covering every guard path.
resources: t605–t608 bug hunt notes (S1–S4 patterns to follow)

tasks.title: A.5/R4 — dev QB inspector (/dev/qb-inspector) to simulate approve/reject/paid
tasks.notes: Port GS /dev/qb-inspector (gated on QB_FAKE). UI to simulate client-approved / rejected estimate + invoice-paid against fake_qb_* tables. Needed to exercise Section F locally.

prep.what: Port the GS `/dev/qb-inspector` page to CT Pros — a `QB_FAKE`-gated UI to trigger approve/reject/paid events against fake QB tables without touching real QuickBooks.
prep.understand: The fake-QB layer intercepts `sync()` calls via the `QB_FAKE` flag and `fake_qb_*` tables. Without this inspector you can't locally test the Section F approval flow (t31) or the payment poller. Last R4 piece of the A.5 GS-pattern adoption.
approach: Find `/dev/qb-inspector` in the GS repo — note the route, controller, and Vue page.
Map GS entities to CT Pros equivalents (clients→customers, documents→estimates/invoices, transactions→payments).
Build the route + controller in CT Pros (behind `APP_ENV=local AND QB_FAKE=true`).
Build the Vue page with three actions: simulate estimate accepted, estimate rejected, invoice paid.
Wire `fake_qb_*` table updates — verify `qb_sync_status` propagates to the entities.

tasks.title: Initial mapping wizard: expense categories → QB accounts, default income account, tax codes, existing-record matching
tasks.notes: After connecting, walk Admin through a 4-step wizard. Persist mappings in settings table. Accessible from Settings → QuickBooks → Edit Mapping anytime.

prep.what: Build the 4-step Settings → QuickBooks mapping wizard: expense categories to QB accounts, default income account, tax code, and optional client/sub matching to existing QB records.
prep.understand: Every sync service reads `qb_category_account_map`, `qb_default_income_account_id`, `qb_tax_code_id` from settings. Without this wizard those settings don't exist — every sync fails at the account-reference lookup. This is the config prerequisite for going live.
approach: Add a 4-step Vue wizard at `/settings/quickbooks/mapping`.
Step 1: fetch QB Accounts via `QuickBooksClient` — dropdown per `ExpenseCategory`.
Steps 2–3: pick default income account, then pick QB `TaxCode`.
Step 4: fuzzy-match CT Pros clients/subs by name to QB Customers/Vendors — confirm and write `qb_id` back.
Persist via `PATCH /settings/quickbooks/mapping` → `Setting::setValue()`.
resources: QB v3 API `/query` (`SELECT * FROM Account WHERE Active=true`)

tasks.title: Sandbox-validate all 6 entity QB mappings + upsert/LinkedTxn shapes
tasks.notes: BLOCKER before production. Connect an Intuit sandbox, validate field mappings, sparse upserts, Payment LinkedTxn shapes, ItemRef requirement on Estimate lines, Purchase TotalAmt output-only.

prep.what: Connect a real Intuit sandbox and fire each of the 6 sync services against it — the fake-QB layer covers flow, but only a real sandbox catches API shape bugs.
prep.understand: Known production risks: `Payment.LinkedTxn` must be an array of objects (not flat); `ItemRef` is REQUIRED on Estimate lines; `Purchase.TotalAmt` is output-only. Any one of these wrong = silent failure in production. This is the go-live BLOCKER.
approach: Create an Intuit sandbox company at developer.intuit.com — grab ClientId/Secret.
Set `QB_CLIENT_ID`, `QB_CLIENT_SECRET`, `QB_ENV=sandbox` in local `.env`; run `php artisan qb:connect` to get tokens.
Create test entities and trigger each sync job via `tinker`: Customer → Vendor → Estimate → Invoice → Payment → Expense.
Check the sandbox dashboard for each: record created? Fields correct?
Fix mapping errors and add unit tests (`Http::fake()` against QBO URLs).
resources: developer.intuit.com — sandbox company + OAuth app setup
QB v3 API field reference (Payment LinkedTxn shape, Purchase TotalAmt note)
t29 notes listing the specific known risks
